promptBudgetPolicy.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.17: Optional hard prompt-budget policy. Absent = no rejection (true no-op). When declared, the final assembled prompt is checked against maxBytes and/or maxTokens before the first model request. Precedence: action > state > settings.

maxBytes.description: Maximum UTF-8 byte length for the final assembled prompt. When exceeded, the worker fails before the model request.

maxTokens.description: Maximum estimated token count for the final assembled prompt (estimate = ceil(byteLength / 4)). When exceeded, the worker fails before the model request.

route.description: Deterministic outcome route emitted when the limit is exceeded. Must be a declared outcome in the statechart vocabulary (startup lint rejects unknown routes).

toolPayloadBudgetPolicy.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.18: Optional hard tool-payload budget policy. Absent = no rejection for that result path (true no-op). When declared, the model-facing tool result is checked against maxBytes before being returned to the model.

maxBytes.description: Maximum UTF-8 byte length for the model-facing tool result payload. When exceeded, the harness rejects the payload and returns a semantic rejection message instead.

route.description: Deterministic outcome route emitted when the limit is exceeded. Must be a declared outcome in the statechart vocabulary (startup lint rejects unknown routes).

runtimeBudgetPolicy.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.48: Optional per-bead/per-state/per-action runtime budget policy. Absent = no enforcement (true no-op, AC1). When declared, exceeded hard limits fail BEFORE the next model/provider/tool spend and route through the configured deterministic outcome. Precedence: action > state > settings (AC2). LLM is NOT asked to self-report budget compliance (AC8).

maxModelCalls.description: Maximum total model-request count across the run. Fails BEFORE the next request when reached.

maxEstimatedInputTokens.description: Maximum cumulative estimated input tokens (ceil(bytes/4)). Checked before each provider request.

maxProviderTotalTokens.description: Maximum cumulative provider-reported total tokens. Checked before each provider request.

maxWallClockMs.description: Maximum wall-clock elapsed milliseconds since worker-run start. Uses injected Clock (deterministic in tests).

maxRetries.description: Maximum cumulative retry count across all tool invocations. Checked before admitting each retry.

maxToolFailures.description: Maximum cumulative per-tool failure count. Checked after each tool failure.

maxVerifierFailures.description: Maximum cumulative verifier-gate rejection count. Checked after each gate rejection.

maxToolPayloadBytes.description: Maximum cumulative tool-result payload bytes sent to the model. Checked before each tool result.

route.description: Deterministic outcome route emitted when any limit is exceeded. Must be a declared outcome in the statechart vocabulary (AC6 startup lint rejects unknown routes).

toolPromptProfileEntry.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.4: A single per-tool prompt text override within a named tool prompt profile.

tool.description: Name of the configured project tool this override applies to. Must match a name in config.tools.

id.description: Profile ID — must match the enclosing profile map key.

text.description: Replacement description injected into the assembled prompt for this tool when the profile is selected. Max 700 characters. Must not contain volatile template placeholders (e.g. {{beadId}}).

retryPolicy.description: pi-experiment-t6gw: Opt-in retry policy. Absent = zero automatic retries. When present, the harness retry pipeline consults sideEffectContract.idempotencyClass before admitting any retry. non_idempotent tools are never retried.

maxAttempts.description: Total attempts including the first (1 = no retry; 2 = one retry; etc.).

retriableCategories.description: Closed set of ToolFailureCategory values that may trigger a retry.

sideEffectContract.description: zog2.9: Deterministic side-effect/resource contract. Required for tools with serialize:true (serializationKey must be non-empty).

cancellationPolicy.description: Whether the tool honours AbortSignal cancellation.

serializationKey.description: Non-null string means tools with this key must not run concurrently. MUST be non-empty when serialize:true. null means no cross-tool serialization constraint.

allowedInReadOnlyContext.description: false means this tool is rejected in review/read-only statechart action contexts.

safeForReadinessProbe.description: false means this tool must not be called during a harness readiness probe.

toolProfileShape.description: Reusable partial command-tool configuration. Fields here are applied as defaults before per-tool values. Per-tool fields always win.

version.description: pi-experiment-202g: Config schema version. When present, must be 2. Absent = v1 (backward-compatible). Triggers v2 admission: rejects removed v1 fields with path-specific diagnostics.

settings.description: All settings fields have ConfigLoader defaults; none are strictly required in the YAML file. Omitted fields are filled from harness defaults before validation.

templates.description: Artifact-type registry. Each entry is a path template (string shorthand) or a rich declaration object. Object form lets an extension declare a harness-generated, writable system artifact.

scope.description: Root the template resolves against when relative. Defaults to project.

writable.description: Permit the teammate to write this artifact's exact resolved path outside the plan write set.

ensureDir.description: Harness ensures the artifact's parent directory exists (mkdir -p).

roots.description: Named path roots resolved at runtime and injected as {{roots.NAME}} template variables into tool env, prompt variables, and path normalization virtualRoots. Values are absolute paths or paths relative to projectRoot. Keys are generic identifiers — project-specific names stay in project config, not harness defaults.

toolDefaults.description: Global defaults applied to every command tool before per-tool or per-profile values. Per-tool fields always win over toolDefaults.

toolProfiles.description: Named reusable partial command-tool configuration blocks. A command tool may reference one profile by name via its 'profile' field; the profile's fields are applied after toolDefaults and before per-tool fields.

toolPromptProfiles.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.4: Named per-tool prompt profile declarations. Each entry maps a profile name to an array of per-tool text overrides. Profile entries replace individual tool descriptions in the assembled prompt. Startup lint enforces: unknown tool names, unknown profile IDs, duplicate entries, volatile templates, and 700-char text limit.

toolPromptProfile.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.4: Settings-level default tool prompt profile. Applied to all states/actions that do not declare their own toolPromptProfile. Must reference a key in settings.toolPromptProfiles. Overridden by state and action-level toolPromptProfile declarations.

promptBudget.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.17: Settings-level (global) prompt-budget policy. Lowest precedence — applies to all states/actions that do not declare their own promptBudget. Absent = no limit enforced anywhere (true no-op). State and action-level declarations override this.

promptBudgetStateOverrides.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.17 AC7(b): Named per-state budget overrides keyed by state ID. Startup lint rejects keys that do not match a declared state (AC7(b) unknown-state-reference check). Precedence: lower than state.promptBudget, higher than settings.promptBudget.

promptBudgetActionOverrides.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.17 AC7(b): Named per-action budget overrides keyed by 'stateId/actionId'. Startup lint rejects keys whose state segment is not a declared state, or whose action segment is not a declared action of that state (AC7(b) unknown-action-reference check). Precedence: lower than action.promptBudget, higher than settings.promptBudgetStateOverrides.

toolPayloadBudget.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.18: Default tool-payload budget applied to all tools that do not declare an explicit per-tool budget. Absent = no limit enforced anywhere (true no-op, AC2). Per-tool declarations in toolPayloadBudgetByTool override this default.

toolPayloadBudgetByTool.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.18 AC4/AC7: Per-tool payload budget overrides keyed by tool name. Takes precedence over settings.toolPayloadBudget (default). Startup lint (AC7) rejects keys that do not match a declared tool name and routes absent from the statechart vocabulary.

runtimeBudget.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.48: Settings-level (global) runtime budget policy. Lowest precedence — applies when no state or action runtimeBudget is configured. Absent = no runtime enforcement anywhere (full no-op, AC1). State and action-level declarations override this.

tsProjectToolDefaults.description: Harness-wide defaults for tsProjectTool shorthand tools. scriptDir is the base directory (relative to projectRoot) used to resolve the default script path: <scriptDir>/<toolName>.ts. Defaults to .pi/project-tools.

scriptDir.description: Base directory for default TS project-tool scripts. Relative to projectRoot. Defaults to .pi/project-tools.

argsMode.description: Default argsMode for tsProjectTool tools. Defaults to append.

allowArgs.description: Default allowArgs for tsProjectTool tools. Defaults to true.

worktreePolicy.description: Harness-wide worktree allocation policy. Controls which states receive an isolated git worktree before the teammate is spawned. When absent, defaults to { default: 'always' }, preserving pre-existing behavior. Per-state provisionWorktree overrides this policy for individual states.

default.description: Default provisioning mode for all states without an explicit provisionWorktree override. 'always' provisions a worktree for every state (default); 'never' runs all states at the project root unless overridden per-state.

cycleCap.description: Maximum consecutive self-loop cycles before a blocked bead is escalated to the Team Lead.

retention.description: Retention policy for harness-owned log/.tmp/.trash areas and event-JSONL compaction.

maxAgeMs.description: Maximum age in milliseconds for harness-owned log/.tmp/.trash entries. Defaults to 2 days.

compactionEnabled.description: Whether event-JSONL compaction is enabled. Defaults to false (safe default).

compactionWindowMs.description: Age in ms after which non-replay-critical events may be compacted. Defaults to 7 days.

diskHealthWarnBytes.description: Bytes threshold for emitting a RETENTION_DISK_HEALTH event. Defaults to 50 MiB.

tools.description: Configured project tools. v1: array of objects with required 'name' field. v2 (pi-experiment-0dgy): map form accepted in YAML (ConfigLoader normalizes to sorted array before schema validation); map keys are the canonical tool names (identifier grammar: ^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_.-]*$).

profile.description: Name of a settings.toolProfiles entry whose fields are applied as defaults for this tool (after toolDefaults, before per-tool fields).

rootKind.description: Built-in values: worktree, project, framework, workspace. Any key from settings.roots is also valid and resolves to the configured named root.

cacheable.description: When true, the harness memoises this tool's results within a single worker session keyed by (toolName, params). Intended for stable read-only structural queries.

serialize.description: Serialize invocations of this command tool across teammates via the same generic cross-process lock that serializes MCP tools (for stateful tools that must not run concurrently).

probeContext.description: When true, this tool is executed as a readiness probe at startup before model spend. Requires sideEffectContract.safeForReadinessProbe: true (pi-experiment-8ieq).

cacheable.description: When true, the harness memoises this tool's results within a single worker session keyed by (toolName, params). Intended for stable read-only structural queries.

serialize.description: Serialize calls to this MCP tool across teammates via a cross-process lock (for stateful/non-concurrent-safe MCP servers).

probeContext.description: When true, this tool is executed as a readiness probe at startup before model spend. Requires sideEffectContract.safeForReadinessProbe: true (pi-experiment-8ieq).

rootKind.description: Built-in values: worktree, project, framework, workspace. Any key from settings.roots is also valid and resolves to the configured named root.

cacheable.description: When true, the harness memoises this tool's results within a single worker session keyed by (toolName, params). Intended for stable read-only structural queries.

observeOnly.description: When true, this extension tool is declared for observation only. The harness records its calls but does not enforce Pi host inventory presence, callable status, or provenance at startup. observeOnly tools cannot satisfy requiredTools.

oneOf.description: TypeScript project-tool shorthand. Expands to type: command with command: node, --experimental-strip-types, and the resolved script path before schema validation. argsMode defaults to append and allowArgs defaults to true unless explicitly overridden.

scriptPath.description: Path to the TypeScript script. Absolute or relative to projectRoot. Defaults to <settings.tsProjectToolDefaults.scriptDir>/<toolName>.ts (or .pi/project-tools/<toolName>.ts if not configured). Supports {{projectRoot}} template variable.

profile.description: Name of a settings.toolProfiles entry applied after tsProjectTool defaults and before per-tool fields.

defaultArgs.description: Additional args appended after the script path. The node + --experimental-strip-types + scriptPath prefix is always prepended.

cacheable.description: When true, the harness memoises this tool's results within a single worker session keyed by (toolName, params). Intended for stable read-only structural queries.

serialize.description: Serialize invocations of this tsProjectTool across teammates via the same generic cross-process lock that serializes MCP tools (survives the tsProjectTool to command expansion).

probeContext.description: When true, this tool is executed as a readiness probe at startup before model spend. Requires sideEffectContract.safeForReadinessProbe: true (pi-experiment-8ieq).

statechart.description: Statechart vocabulary configuration. All fields except terminalStates have defaults that reproduce the original SUCCESS/FAILURE/BLOCKED/completed literals.

initialState.description: v1: Override for the initial state (mirrors settings.startState; settings wins). Rejected in version:2 configs — use statechart.initial instead.

terminalStates.description: v1: State IDs that are terminal (workflow is done when reached). Defaults to ['completed'] when the block is absent. Rejected in version:2 configs — use statechart.terminal instead.

initial.description: v2 (pi-experiment-202g): Names the single runnable start state. Used in version:2 configs instead of v1 initialState.

terminal.description: v2 (pi-experiment-202g): Lists terminal sink names that must not also be runnable states. Used in version:2 configs instead of v1 terminalStates.

advanceOutcomes.description: Outcome strings that trigger forward state advancement. Defaults to ['SUCCESS'].

failedOutcomes.description: Outcome strings that map to STATE_FAILED. Defaults to ['FAILURE'].

blockedOutcomes.description: Outcome strings that map to STATE_BLOCKED. Defaults to ['BLOCKED'].

customOutcomes.description: Additional custom outcome strings beyond the named sets.

customEvents.description: Placeholder: custom event names whose behaviour is filled by a parallel bead.

events.description: pi-experiment-cfzu: v2 category-first event vocabulary. Only valid in version:2 configs. Each event name is canonical (UPPER_SNAKE_CASE) and appears in exactly one category after case-insensitive normalization. Categories are TAXONOMY ONLY — category membership never supplies a default transition, failure route, or fallback status. A state routes an event only when it declares an exact transition key for the canonical event name.

advance.description: Advance-category event names. Taxonomy only — no routing fallback from category membership.

failure.description: Failure-category event names. Taxonomy only — no routing fallback from category membership.

blocked.description: Blocked-category event names. Taxonomy only — no routing fallback from category membership.

neutral.description: Neutral-category event names. Taxonomy only — no routing fallback from category membership.

validationGates.description: Configured validation gates. v1: array of objects with required 'id' field. v2 (pi-experiment-0dgy): map form accepted in YAML (ConfigLoader normalizes to sorted array before schema validation); map keys are the canonical gate IDs (identifier grammar: ^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_.-]*$).

operator.description: pi-experiment-ne2w: v2 gate aggregation operator. allOf = pass only if all checks pass. anyOf = pass if at least one check passes. noneOf is out of scope.

checks.description: pi-experiment-ne2w: ordered list of checks evaluated by this gate (no short-circuit).

checkId.description: Stable ID of the tool or verifier to evaluate.

passEvent.description: Event name emitted when this check passes. Must be in the v2 vocabulary.

failEvent.description: Event name emitted when this check fails. Must be in the v2 vocabulary.

blockedEvent.description: Event name emitted when this check is blocked. Optional. Must be in the v2 vocabulary if present.

passEvent.description: pi-experiment-ne2w: event name emitted when the gate resolves to pass (operator succeeds). Must be in the v2 vocabulary.

failPrecedence.description: pi-experiment-ne2w: ordered precedence list for failure-category events (highest priority first). Required when two or more checks can emit different failure events. Each possible failure event must appear exactly once.

blockPrecedence.description: pi-experiment-ne2w: ordered precedence list for blocked-category events (highest priority first). Required when two or more checks can emit different blocked events. Each possible blocked event must appear exactly once.

states.description: Statechart states. Keys are canonical state IDs. v2 (pi-experiment-0dgy): state keys must match the v2 identifier grammar (^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_.-]*$). Each state's actions may be declared as a map in v2 (ConfigLoader normalizes to sorted array with canonical IDs before schema validation).

expectsVerify.description: When true, the coordinator artifact-presence gate fails fast at startup if no verify() callback is registered for this tool. Presence-only tools omit this flag.

provisionWorktree.description: Whether to provision an isolated git worktree for this state. When true, the Supervisor creates a per-bead worktree before spawning the teammate. When false, the teammate runs at the project root. When absent, falls back to settings.worktreePolicy.default ('always' if not set).

activeTools.description: Explicit set of tool names active for this state. When absent, all tools in config.tools are active (default: full exposure). Action-level activeTools further narrows this set. Unknown names fail startup lint.

toolPromptProfile.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.4: State-level tool prompt profile. Overrides settings.toolPromptProfile for this state and all its actions (unless an action declares its own toolPromptProfile). Must reference a key in settings.toolPromptProfiles.

promptBudget.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.17: State-level prompt-budget policy. When present, all actions in this state inherit this budget unless the action declares its own promptBudget (action > state > settings precedence). Startup lint rejects negative limits and routes absent from the statechart vocabulary.

runtimeBudget.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.48: State-level runtime budget policy. Applies to all actions in this state unless the action declares its own runtimeBudget (action > state > settings precedence, AC2). Absent = falls back to settings.runtimeBudget. AC6 startup lint rejects negative limits and routes absent from the statechart vocabulary.

routeEvidence.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.46: Per-route required-tool evidence. Maps outcome names to required-tool arrays. When a route event matches, the coordinator gate enforces these tools before applying the transition. Startup lint fails if any tool with expectsVerify:true has no registered verify() callback.

expectsVerify.description: When true, startup lint fails if no verify() callback is registered for this tool.

contextPolicy.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.44: State-level context policy. Declares how this state's worker context is handled at spawn time. Default when absent: freshSubagent (new isolated sub-agent context). String shorthand: freshSubagent or namedContinuation (namedContinuation requires structured form with contextKey). Structured form: { mode, contextKey }.

oneOf.description: String shorthand for the context policy mode. Use 'freshSubagent' for the default or 'namedContinuation' requires the structured form with a contextKey.

contextKey.description: Stable key naming the context to continue. Required when mode=namedContinuation. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, dashes, and underscores.

producesContextKey.description: Stable key under which this state's Pi session is stored for a subsequent namedContinuation state to resume. When set the coordinator spawns this state with a persistent Pi session and records the path in the contextKeyStore. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, dashes, and underscores.

actions.description: State actions. v1: array of objects with required 'id' and 'type' fields. v2 (pi-experiment-0dgy): map form accepted in YAML (ConfigLoader normalizes to sorted array before schema validation); map keys are the canonical action IDs (identifier grammar: ^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_.-]*$).

toolPromptProfile.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.4: Action-level tool prompt profile (highest precedence). Overrides state.toolPromptProfile and settings.toolPromptProfile for this action. Must reference a key in settings.toolPromptProfiles.

promptBudget.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.17: Action-level prompt-budget policy (highest precedence). Overrides state.promptBudget and settings.promptBudget for this action. Startup lint rejects negative limits and routes absent from the statechart vocabulary.

runtimeBudget.description: pi-experiment-6q0y.48: Action-level runtime budget policy (highest precedence, AC2). Overrides state.runtimeBudget and settings.runtimeBudget for this specific action. AC6 startup lint rejects negative limits and routes absent from the statechart vocabulary.

llm.description: pi-experiment-0njv: v2 LLM action configuration. When present, this action is a v2 LLM action. llm.promptFile is required; inline prompt bodies (llm.prompt or top-level prompt) are forbidden. Path safety is enforced at config load before any provider/model request.

promptFile.description: Project-relative path to the prompt file. Must be a non-empty normalized path within the project root (no absolute paths, no .. escape, no symlink escape, no directory, no unreadable/nonexistent file).

v2PromptProvenance.description: pi-experiment-0njv: Resolved prompt provenance for this v2 LLM action. Populated by ConfigLoader at load time. Records normalized path, byteCount, sha256 digest, and actionId. The prompt body is never stored here.

emits.description: pi-experiment-hutg: v2 action route-event emits mapping. Config-owned mapping from deterministic verdicts (pass/fail/blocked/preconditionFailed) to declared v2 event names. Only valid on tool/verifier actions. LLM actions (with llm block) may not declare emits. All event names must be in the declared v2 vocabulary. Startup rejects LLM emitters, undeclared event refs, and missing preconditionFailed when the action requires artifacts.

pass.description: Event name emitted when the deterministic verdict is pass. Must be in the declared v2 event vocabulary.

fail.description: Event name emitted when the deterministic verdict is fail. Must be in the declared v2 event vocabulary.

blocked.description: Event name emitted when the deterministic verdict is blocked. Optional. Must be in the declared v2 event vocabulary.

preconditionFailed.description: Event name emitted BEFORE tool/verifier body when a required artifact is missing. If absent and the action requires artifacts, startup rejects the action.
